output:
CVE-2022-42967 describes a critical vulnerability in Caret, a text editor, where an attacker can execute a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attack. This vulnerability occurs when a user opens a specifically crafted Markdown file while in preview mode, enabling client-side code execution. XSS is identified under CWE-79, which indicates improper neutralization of user inputs before placing it in web page outputs.

The severity level of this vulnerability is classified as CRITICAL, highlighting immediate risks of code execution on targeted systems. Affected software includes Caret versions that match the criteria specified in its Common Platform Enumeration (CPE).

This vulnerability poses significant risks, primarily allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code on an affected user’s system. The MITRE ATT&CK framework classifies this under technique T1203, reflecting potential exploitation through user interaction with malicious files.

Mitigation techniques involve M1050, M1051, and M1048, which include employing rigorous input validation, leveraging security controls to protect user sessions, and ensuring application updates are applied promptly to address the vulnerability.
